body: Most people don't have a communication problem. They have a clarity problem. Fix that first, and everything else gets easier.
How many times have you hit 'delete' instead of 'enter' on a message? Too vulnerable. Too much. Too messy. I know, me too. And here's why you should send it anyway.
Read the room. Tone it down. Not the right time. Honestly, that's some of the worst well-intentioned advice we all seem to regurgitate ad nauseum.
The research is clear: people don't remember what you said. They remember how you made them feel. Which means most of us are optimizing for the wrong thing entirely.
I sat in that boardroom for twenty minutes before I said a word. Not because I didn't have anything to say. Because I was waiting to feel ready. That was the day I realized 'ready' was never coming.
If this resonates, share it with someone who needs to hear it. And if you're ready to stop shrinking your voice, let's talk.
So here's what I keep coming back to: what would you say if you knew the right people were actually listening?
